codeReview.summary: The missing `close` emission is restored, but commit `9bf6a3e` also changes user-facing copy outside the feature scope, so the implementation does not cleanly match the assigned regression fix.

issues.description: The feature changes `selectedComponentSummary` from `已选中 N 个 BRAN 管道构件` to `已选中 N 个构件`, which is unrelated to restoring the missing `close` emission. This broadens the fix beyond its stated scope and changes existing UX copy without test coverage for the summary text.

The success notification details were rewritten to a generic progress hint and dropped the reviewer/approver plus attachment-count information previously shown to users. That copy change is unrelated to the close-event regression and unnecessarily increases feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `designer-tracking-worker` procedure requires agent-browser manual verification, but this worker explicitly skipped it for a narrow component-event regression and relied on focused Vitest coverage plus full-suite validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/designer-tracking-worker/SKILL.md step 6 requires manual verification; handoff.skillFeedback.deviations for worker `c33c2f43-583e-4c76-a52f-4ef956bb9a32` says browser automation was intentionally not run because the issue was an emitted-event regression already covered by tests.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit `9bf6a3e`. The commit does fix the missing `close` emission after successful submit, but it also introduces out-of-scope UX copy changes in `src/components/review/InitiateReviewPanel.vue`, including the selected-component summary string later reverted by follow-up feature `revert-out-of-scope-copy-change-in-form-binding-fix`. Because this feature should have been limited to the close-event regression, the review remains a fail.
